title: Quality Review — checking project claims against evidence, scope, and readiness
public_claim: A quality-review and hardening surface for moving projects toward release.
distinctive_mechanism: It asks what is built, what claim it supports, what evidence proves it, what can break, and what demonstration would make the claim stronger, moving projects through Stage S0 Prospecting Build, Stage S1 Survey, Stage S2 Lite, Deep Anchored work, Stage S3 SOPs, red-team executable SOPs, Stage S4 Yield, Stage S5 Pilot Pre-register, RETIRE Validation, committee-facing S3 readiness, release freeze, redundancy review, and sign-off.
use_value: It is the anti-overclaim room: it turns ambition into staged proof obligations, release criteria, and review gates.

flagship_proof_builds.name: Claim-to-scope hardening proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: Quality Review should show how a project claim is narrowed until it matches evidence, status, audience, and readiness.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one public project claim, supporting artifacts, scope limit, audience, and status record
flagship_proof_builds.operation: compare the claim against evidence, implementation state, source support, and release risk
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: claim hardening decision: keep, narrow, source, defer, or remove
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: select one overbroad claim and show the exact reason it is kept, narrowed, deferred, or removed

flagship_proof_builds.name: Completion-language restraint proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: A project should not be called built, complete, validated, published, or deployed unless the proof state supports that word.
flagship_proof_builds.input: status sentence, component list, evidence file, runtime proof, and external-review status
flagship_proof_builds.operation: map public wording to proof tier and block stronger language when evidence is missing
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: approved public wording plus blocked stronger alternatives
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: test one status paragraph and show which words are permitted, which are blocked, and why

component_proofs.claim_supported: opens the claim as a review object rather than treating it as finished prose; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_proofs.input: draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience
component_proofs.expected_output: claim record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: claim record as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (draft claim, source, counter-source, intended audience) produces output (claim record as a reviewable artifact) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: uses Claim Halo / review chamber logic to expose what authorizes or weakens the claim
component_proofs.input: source strength, contradiction, dissent, revision state
component_proofs.expected_output: claim ceiling and revision decision as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: claim ceiling and revision decision as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (source strength, contradiction, dissent, revision state) produces output (claim ceiling and revision decision as a reviewable...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: decides whether the claim can publish, must narrow, needs sources, or should be rejected
component_proofs.expected_output: certified claim or revision task as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: certified claim or revision task as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (reviewed claim and evidence state) produces output (certified claim or revision task as a reviewable...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

minimum_public_demo: select one overbroad claim and show the exact reason it is kept, narrowed, deferred, or removed
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
